title: USD Sublayer Loading Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: The pinned composition, document_data_model, file_formats, and resource-interface specifications are normative. This ledger covers bounded recursive local sublayer resource loading.

status_values.implemented: In scope for the layer-stack-open contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.partial: A bounded local subset is included and later contracts must extend it.
status_values.deferred: Specified behavior intentionally not included in this first loading contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: The loader opens resources and delegates namespace composition to usd-layer-stack-namespace-source.
The resolver policy is local and relative to the layer that authored each subLayer, not full USD asset resolution.
A generated target must not claim package loading or full asset resolver support from this provider.

obligations: Open the root layer through usd-layer-open.
Return a target-native result, not a dump JSON tree.
Read the root layer's direct subLayers.
Resolve direct local sublayer identifiers relative to the root resource.
items.deferred_reason: Full asset resolver behavior remains separate from this local prototype policy.

obligations: Report missing sublayer resources clearly at any recursive stack depth.
Include the authored asset identifier in diagnostics.
Do not construct a successful layer-stack source when required sublayers fail.
Unsupported URI schemes must not silently fall back to local filesystem paths.
Read subLayers from every successfully opened local layer.
Resolve nested relative sublayer identifiers relative to the resource location of the layer that authored them.
Open recursively discovered local sublayers through usd-layer-open.
Insert nested sublayers depth-first immediately after the sublayer that authored them and before later siblings.
Detect cycles by resolved layer location, report a diagnostic, and avoid unbounded recursion.

items.deferred_reason: The existing resource protocol models package spelling, but package sublayer loading and inner-root selection remain out of scope here.
